causes: Make sure the NAND and surrounding components are properly connected and seated.
Check for damaged, missing, shorted, or liquid-damaged components around the NAND circuit.
Check the logic board for cracks, separation, or prior repair damage.
Diode-mode the NAND-related test points and circuits for abnormal readings.
If the fault persists, the NAND may require reballing, reprogramming, or replacement by a qualified microsoldering technician.
